articles.title: Micron, Intel and other chip stocks fall up to 11% after record-breaking rally
articles.summary: Semiconductor stocks opened Q3 with sharp profit-taking — the VanEck Semiconductor ETF plunged over 5%, led by Micron, Intel and AMD — as Meta's plans fueled fears of AI computing oversupply. Matters for trading: the same AI-capacity scare that hit Korea's KOSPI is now hitting US chips — watch Indian IT and any AI-linked names for contagion.

articles.title: No longer magnificent? Mag 7 stocks crumbling under AI pressure
articles.summary: The Magnificent Seven — Apple, Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Nvidia, Tesla — are underperforming for the first time since the AI rally began, with $2.3 trillion in value erased in June as investors demand proof that massive AI spending converts to profits. Matters for trading: earnings season now pivots on AI monetisation, not AI hype — a regime change for global tech exposure.

articles.title: India bonds surge on Bloomberg index hopes, oil-led inflation relief
articles.summary: Indian government bonds rallied Thursday on strong foreign buying, hopes of inclusion in Bloomberg's bond index, and easing inflation expectations as oil prices fall. Matters for trading: falling yields support rate-sensitive sectors — banks, autos, real estate — and signal FII confidence returning to Indian debt.

articles.title: Adani Enterprises launches QIP, sets floor price at Rs 3,034.68 per share
articles.summary: Adani Enterprises opened its qualified institutional placement to raise funds from institutional investors, fixing the floor price at Rs 3,034.68 per share. Matters for trading: large QIPs can pressure the stock near-term on dilution but signal institutional appetite for Adani group paper.

articles.title: Tesla stock drops 8% despite record second-quarter deliveries
articles.summary: Tesla fell 8.3% to $390 even after reporting record Q2 deliveries of 480,126 vehicles, up 25% year-over-year — the market sold the news amid broader tech weakness. Matters for trading: when record deliveries can't hold a stock up, positioning is stretched — a caution signal for high-multiple EV and tech names.

articles.title: US labor force participation falls to lowest in 50 years outside Covid era
articles.summary: The US unemployment rate fell to 4.2% in June — but for the wrong reasons: workers exited the labor force en masse, dragging participation to 61.5%, the lowest since March 2021 and, excluding Covid, the lowest in exactly 50 years. Matters for trading: a weakening US labor market strengthens the case for Fed easing — supportive for emerging-market flows.

articles.title: With Hormuz reopened, has the oil shortage turned into a glut?
articles.summary: The Strait of Hormuz — handling a fifth of global oil supply — is reopening faster than expected after the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, sending oil down ~1% for a third straight day. Analysts now ask whether shortage flips to surplus. Matters for trading: an oil glut is a sustained tailwind for India — importers, aviation, paints, OMCs — and for the rupee.

articles.title: Carlsberg files confidential IPO papers for India unit
articles.summary: Danish brewer Carlsberg confidentially filed draft IPO papers with Sebi for its Indian business, joining Hyundai Motor and LG Electronics in the wave of multinationals listing Indian subsidiaries to unlock value. The confidential route keeps commercial details private during Sebi review.

articles.title: Tata Motors PV sales jump 46% in Q1; JLR volumes hit by supply disruptions
articles.summary: Tata Motors sold 1,82,574 passenger vehicles in the June quarter, up 46% year-on-year on strong new-launch demand and record EV sales — a strong FY27 start. Jaguar Land Rover volumes fell on supply disruptions, Middle East conflict effects and the planned Jaguar model transition.

articles.title: Meta confirms push into cloud computing — Wall Street braces for lower margins
articles.summary: Meta will sell excess computing power to outside customers, entering cloud infrastructure against AWS, Microsoft and Google. The debate inside Meta: offer hosted AI models or raw compute. Cloud is lucrative but carries far slimmer margins than Meta's ad business — a structural shift in how it monetises its massive AI buildout.

articles.title: Ford CEO wants level playing field with Toyota, GM imports as USMCA talks reopen
articles.summary: As USMCA renegotiation formally opens, Ford CEO Jim Farley wants domestically-producing automakers rewarded and import-heavy rivals like GM and Toyota penalised. Follows the US blocking the pact's automatic 16-year renewal — auto supply chains across North America now face prolonged uncertainty.

articles.title: How the US-Iran war may push Gulf countries to diversify security alliances
articles.summary: GCC nations that came under Iranian attack during the war are accelerating moves to diversify security partnerships beyond the US, analysts say, even as Tehran-Washington talks continue. A structural shift in Gulf geopolitics with long-run implications for energy security and defence procurement.

articles.title: German prosecutors accuse Kyiv of ordering 2022 Nord Stream sabotage
articles.summary: German prosecutors formally accused Ukrainian state authorities of ordering the Nord Stream pipeline attack, indicting suspect Serhiy Kuznietsov — a charge likely to inflame tensions between Kyiv and Berlin, Ukraine's biggest military backer in Europe. A major escalation from yesterday's individual indictment.

articles.title: Fears of new massacre in Sudan's el-Obeid as RSF tightens siege
articles.summary: The UN Human Rights Council holds an urgent debate Friday on el-Obeid, where the RSF is besieging 500,000 civilians with drone strikes on residential areas and reinforcements massing outside. International groups fear a repeat of the el-Fasher mass killings documented by Amnesty.

articles.title: Bomb blast at Damascus cafe kills six near Palace of Justice
articles.summary: An explosive device planted inside a crowded cafe 100m from Damascus's Palace of Justice killed at least six and injured 22 — the deadliest bombing in the capital since the June 2025 church attack. No group has claimed it; Syria's new state is struggling to enforce security.

articles.title: Anthropic in talks with Samsung over custom AI chip
articles.summary: Anthropic is discussing a custom chip collaboration with Samsung, The Information reports — getting serious about the in-house silicon idea first floated in April as a chip-shortage hedge. Specs and role undecided; Anthropic says a diversified stack of Google, Amazon and Nvidia chips remains central. Follows OpenAI's Broadcom chip partnership last week.

articles.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
articles.summary: Sam Altman proposed giving 5% of OpenAI's equity to a US sovereign wealth fund, per the FT, with other AI companies donating similar stakes — a move to secure relations with the administration and blunt political blowback. Trump previously confirmed discussing 'pieces given to the American public'. Unprecedented state-AI entanglement if it happens.

articles.title: Microsoft launches AI deployment company with $2.5 billion and 6,000 experts
articles.summary: Microsoft announced 'Microsoft Frontier Company', an operating business with $2.5B backing and 6,000 engineers focused on delivering enterprise AI deployments with Microsoft's AI tools. Follows Amazon, OpenAI and Anthropic in the forward-deployed-engineering trend — the AI fight is shifting from models to implementation.

articles.title: Meta quietly launches vibe-coded gaming app Pocket
articles.summary: Meta released Pocket, an experimental app where users generate and share interactive mini-games ('gizmos') from AI text prompts, built from its acquisition of vibe-coding platform Gizmo. Includes a scrollable feed of others' creations — Meta testing AI-generated content as a consumer entertainment format.

articles.title: Boeing tests 737 MAX 10 autoland in extreme crosswinds for certification
articles.summary: Boeing flight crews deliberately chased strong gusting conditions over Midland International to prove the 737 MAX 10's autoland capability in extreme crosswinds — one of the toughest tests on its certification path. Certification progress matters: the MAX 10 is years late and airlines worldwide have big orders riding on it.

articles.title: American launches 10 longest routes from Chicago, up to 6,200-mile nonstops
articles.summary: American Airlines launched seven new long-haul services from Chicago O'Hare this year with three more coming — together its ten longest from the hub. Seven go to Europe, one across the Pacific (the 6,200-mile Tokyo Narita service returning March 2027), plus two domestic. A notable hub-strategy expansion at ORD.

articles.title: Emirates abruptly cuts A380 flights to Heathrow in July
articles.summary: Emirates is swapping Boeing 777-300ERs onto some Dubai-Heathrow services in July, trimming A380 frequency on the world's busiest superjumbo route — partly a knock-on from the Iran war's effect on Gulf operations. Route returned to six daily flights July 1 after running five in June.

articles.title: 'Miracle': trapped man rescued eight days after Venezuela earthquakes
articles.summary: A security guard was pulled alive from a collapsed building eight days after twin earthquakes devastated Venezuela, as focus shifts from rescue to a humanitarian crisis: 2,295+ confirmed dead, 13,000 homeless, and an estimated 60,000 buildings damaged or destroyed.

articles.title: Côte d'Ivoire floods kill 59 as west Africa endures torrential rains
articles.summary: Floods have killed 59 in Côte d'Ivoire since May with the toll expected to rise, while neighbouring Ghana reports 13 dead and Benin, Togo and Nigeria are also hit. Authorities warn the relentless coastal west African rains could intensify in coming days.

articles.title: China says pilot crashed plane into Beijing skyscraper for 'personal reasons'
articles.summary: Chinese authorities concluded the pilot who flew a small plane into Beijing's tallest skyscraper last week — killing himself and wounding 13 — acted for personal reasons, citing chronic insomnia, anxiety and diary references to ending his life. Officials ruled it endangerment of public safety, not terrorism.

articles.title: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker arrested in Finland, extradited to US
articles.summary: A 19-year-old was arrested in Finland and extradited to the US over alleged involvement with Scattered Spider, the hacking group linked to over $100m in ransom payments. Charged with computer intrusion, conspiracy and fraud — follows guilty pleas in the £39m Transport for London cyber-attack case.
